stories.title: Texas 2027 — The Most Loaded Roster in College Softball History
stories.angle: With the portal closed and every major domino settled, Texas 2027 can now be evaluated in full. Isa Torres (.530 BA / .978 SLG / .591 OBP) joins Katie Stewart (31 HRs, program record), Samantha Swan (All-Big 12 catcher from ASU, .383 BA / 12 HR), Teagan Kavan (two-time WCWS MOP), and incoming freshman Madi Doty (No. 2 national pitching prospect). This lineup — returning the core of the team that won back-to-back titles and adding the Player of the Year — may be the most dangerous single-season roster ever assembled in college softball. The three-peat bid has never looked more legitimate.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Texas 2027: Isa Torres (.530 BA / .978 SLG). Katie Stewart (31 HRs). Teagan Kavan. Samantha Swan.

The Longhorns may have just built the most dangerous roster in college softball history. #HookEm

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Teagan Kavan is back. Two-time WCWS MOP.

Now she has Torres (.530 BA), Stewart (31 HRs), Swan (.383/12 HR), and top freshman Madi Doty.

Texas isn't reloading. Texas is escalating. #LonghornSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Let's talk about what Texas just built.

The 2026 transfer portal window is closed. The Longhorns didn't just survive it — they weaponized it.

Isa Torres is coming to Austin. The D1Softball Player of the Year. The ACC Player of the Year. A .530 batting average, .978 slugging percentage, .591 on-base percentage. An NCAA-record 16 consecutive at-bats with a hit. She's a Georgetown, TX native who wanted to come home — and the two-time defending champions were waiting.

Now layer her onto what Texas already had:

Katie Stewart hit 31 home runs in 2026, a new Texas program record. She was the first player in WCWS history to homer in four consecutive games.

Samantha Swan — All-Big 12 catcher out of Arizona State, .383 BA, 12 HRs, 55 RBIs — committed to Texas in June. She's also from Georgetown, Texas.

Teagan Kavan becomes the first two-time WCWS Most Outstanding Pitcher in history. She went 4-1 with a 1.47 ERA and 30 strikeouts across 33.1 innings in the 2026 tournament.

And incoming freshman Madi Doty arrives as one of the top pitching prospects in the 2027 class.

Is this the most dangerous team ever assembled in college softball? It's a real question — and right now, the answer is hard to argue against.

The three-peat bid starts now.

#HookEm #LonghornSoftball #CollegeSoftball
[Posting Window: 9:00 AM ET]

research.facts:
Texas 2027 Roster

- Kavan 2026 WCWS: 4-1 record, 1.47 ERA, 30 Ks, 33.1 IP — [HIGH confidence]

- Samantha Swan: signed Texas from ASU June 11-12, .383 BA / 12 HR / 55 RBI / All-Big 12, Georgetown TX native — [HIGH confidence]

- Texas 2026 national recruiting class ranked No. 4 per D1Softball — includes Annie Abdullah, Jordynn Booe, Josie Busceme, Mia Clemmer, Madi Doty, Aubrey Portie, Stevie South — [HIGH confidence]
- Texas won 2026 WCWS: defeated Texas Tech 7-3 (Game 1) and 4-1 (Game 2) — [HIGH confidence]
**CAUTION:** The "No. 2 national pitching prospect" for Doty was from a single source. Use "top national pitching prospect" or "one of the top pitching recruits in the class" to be safe.

stories.title: FSU Targets Tia Milloy — Seminoles' Last Big Offseason Move
stories.angle: Florida State's offseason rebuild isn't finished. The Seminoles are pursuing Tia Milloy (.353 BA / 4 HR / .706 SLG at Oklahoma), who entered the transfer portal after OU's Super Regional exit. Milloy was a two-year Sooner with two years of eligibility remaining. Adding Milloy would complement the power additions of Ella Dodge (Tennessee, 23 HRs) and Nicole Edmiaston (Stetson, 11 HRs), while Jazzy Francik (ACC Pitcher of the Year) and Ashtyn Danley anchor the roster. FSU's rebuild may be stronger than the headlines suggest.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
FSU's rebuild isn't finished.

The Seminoles are pursuing Tia Milloy (.353 BA / .706 SLG at OU) alongside Ella Dodge and Nicole Edmiaston. Jazzy Francik anchors the pitching.

This roster is more dangerous than it looks. #FSUSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
FSU lost Torres and Beachum — two of the biggest names in the portal.

But: Francik (ACC Pitcher of the Year) returns. Dodge + Edmiaston add 34 combined HRs. Milloy is in play.

Don't count the Seminoles out in 2027. #Noles

facebook.long_form:
Florida State's summer rebuild isn't finished — and that's actually good news for Seminole fans.

The FSU softball program lost the two biggest names in the 2026 transfer portal. Isa Torres went to Texas. Jaysoni Beachum went to Florida. It was a painful 15-day window in Tallahassee.

But the Seminoles have been building back, and there's one more move in play.

FSU is pursuing Tia Milloy, who entered the transfer portal after Oklahoma's Super Regional exit. Milloy hit .353 with four home runs and a .706 slugging percentage in 41 games for the Sooners. She has two years of eligibility remaining. The Seminoles see her as the final piece of their power-hitting overhaul.

Here's what FSU already has for 2027:

Jazzy Francik won ACC Pitcher of the Year. She is the anchor.

Ashtyn Danley is a proven two-way player who returns.

Ella Dodge (Tennessee) committed to Tallahassee, bringing 23 home runs from her time with the Vols.

Nicole Edmiaston (Stetson) adds 11 more.

That's 34 combined home runs from two portal additions alone — more than Torres and Beachum produced together at FSU.

If Milloy joins this group, Florida State will enter 2027 with a roster that's tougher than it looks on paper. Programs built on elite pitching survive. And Francik is elite.

Don't write off the Seminoles just yet.

#FSUSoftball #Noles #CollegeSoftball
[Posting Window: 11:00 AM ET]

stories.title: Mike White Needs Two Coaches — Texas's Hidden Challenge for 2027
stories.angle: Texas is building a historically loaded roster — but Mike White is also hiring. Associate head coach Ehren Earleywine departed for Tennessee following the 2026 championship. Associate head coach Kristen Zaleski is not returning for 2027. White must replace two members of the staff he just won back-to-back titles with. As the defending champion with the nation's top portal class, Texas is an attractive landing spot — but the right hires matter enormously when you're chasing history.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Texas softball is building its most loaded roster ever — and also filling two coaching vacancies.

Earleywine left for Tennessee. Zaleski is departing. Mike White's next hires will shape the 2027 three-peat bid. #LonghornSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Everyone is talking about what Texas's 2027 roster looks like. Let's talk about what the coaching staff looks like.

After back-to-back national championships, Mike White is rebuilding his bench.

Ehren Earleywine — who coached Texas's record-setting offense (99 HRs, Katie Stewart 31 HRs) — left for Tennessee, where he takes an associate head coaching role. Kristen Zaleski, who was promoted to associate head coach before the 2026 season, will not return for 2027.

White retains assistant coach Pattie Ruth Taylor and the framework of a championship program. But two vacancies — on a staff that just won two straight national titles — need to be filled before fall practice begins.

The good news: there's no more attractive coaching destination in college softball right now. Texas finished 2026 as back-to-back champions, led by the most talented incoming roster in the sport's history.

The pressure: the right hires matter. Chemistry in the dugout is as important as talent on the field. White's track record says he makes good decisions — but this one will be watched closely.

One of the quiet storylines of the summer. Worth tracking.

#LonghornSoftball #HookEm #CollegeSoftball
[Posting Window: 1:00 PM ET]

stories.title: Nebraska 2027 — Can the Cornhuskers Return to OKC Without Jordy Frahm?
stories.angle: Nebraska went 51-6 in 2026, made the WCWS semifinals (lost 2-1 to Tennessee in extra innings), and sent Jordy Frahm — USA Softball Collegiate Player of the Year — off as a two-way force. Now Frahm is gone. The question is whether coach Rhonda Revelle-Deifel has enough returning talent and portal additions to put the Cornhuskers back in Oklahoma City in 2027 after their best season since 2013.

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Nebraska went 51-6, made the WCWS semifinals, and sent Jordy Frahm off as national Player of the Year.

Now Frahm is gone. Can Revelle-Deifel's Cornhuskers get back to OKC in 2027? #Huskers #GBR

facebook.long_form:
Nebraska's 2026 season was one for the record books.

The Cornhuskers went 51-6. They made the Women's College World Series for the first time since 2013. They won a walk-off thriller over Tennessee in pool play. They reached the semifinals before falling to the Vols 2-1 in nine innings.

And Jordy Frahm — their two-way superstar — was named USA Softball Collegiate Player of the Year. She hit 19 home runs. She went 20-4 on the mound. She threw 133 pitches in a single WCWS game. She was everything to a Nebraska team that hadn't been to Oklahoma City in more than a decade.

Now Frahm's college career is over.

The question for head coach Rhonda Revelle-Deifel and the Cornhuskers is simple, and also enormous: can this program sustain that level without the player who made it happen?

The evidence for yes: Nebraska built a 27-game win streak. They swept strong opponents. They went 51-6 in total — that kind of dominant record requires depth, not just one great player.

The evidence for concern: replacing a two-way Player of the Year is extraordinarily hard. There's no precedent for finding someone who can do what Frahm did.

The offseason will tell us a lot. Watch for Nebraska's portal activity, their fall camp reports, and whether Revelle-Deifel can restock the pitching staff.

One of 2026's great stories. One of 2027's most compelling questions.

#GBR #Huskers #CollegeSoftball
[Posting Window: 3:00 PM ET]

stories.title: Summer Showcase Season Is Here — The 2027 High School Class Takes Center Stage
stories.angle: The transfer portal window just closed. The 2026 season ended in Oklahoma City with Texas's second straight title. Now the spotlight shifts to summer showcases — Premier Girls Fastpitch Nationals, Top Gun, Triple Crown — where the 2027 high school class will prove (or define) their recruiting value. Programs that struck out in the portal this summer are making their cases to 2028 recruits right now. College softball never truly stops.

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
The portal closed. The WCWS is done.

Now: PGF Nationals, Top Gun, Triple Crown — the 2027 high school class is being evaluated right now.

The next Torres or Frahm is competing at a showcase this summer. #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
The transfer portal window closed June 22. The Women's College World Series ended weeks ago with Texas hoisting the trophy again. And now… the offseason.

But college softball fans know there is no such thing as a true offseason.

Summer showcase season is underway. Premier Girls Fastpitch Nationals, Top Gun, Triple Crown — these are the events where the 2027 high school recruiting class proves its worth. College coaches who spent the last two weeks obsessing over the portal are now turning their eyes to prospects who won't sign until November.

For programs that missed out on the biggest portal names this summer, showcase season is critical. This is where they build for 2028. This is where a future star commits to play for a program that may not be the most famous — but is the best fit.

For softball fans, these are the events worth following. A player catching fire at PGF Nationals could be the next name you see on a D1 roster. The next Torres, the next Frahm, the next Stewart — they're competing at these showcases right now.

Beyond showcases:
— September 1 opens the official contact window for 2028 high school recruits
— Fall practice for college programs begins in August
— The NFCA Convention comes to Atlantic City in December
— The next transfer portal window opens after the 2027 WCWS

College softball never truly stops. The game goes on.

#CollegeSoftball #Softball #Recruiting
[Posting Window: 5:30 PM ET]
